programme.desc: Indonesian news and culture, in English
John Adams (William Daniels), Benjamin Franklin (Howard da Silva) and Thomas Jefferson (Ken Howard) lead the way to the Colonies' Declaration of Independence.
An ambulance driver and a nurse share an ill-fated romance in World War I Italy. Based on Ernest Hemingway's novel.
programme.title: A Farewell to Arms

programme.desc: Burlesque dancer Dixie Daisy is the prime suspect when rival dancers are found strangled with their own stage costumes. Aided by infatuated stand-up comic Biff Brannigan, Dixie must find the killer before she becomes his next victim.
programme.title: Lady of Burlesque

programme.desc: The first manned flight to Mars is a flop, so a space official (Hal Holbrook) fakes it Hollywood-style for the public.
programme.title: Capricorn One

programme.desc: A bomber pilot (Spencer Tracy) goes to heaven and becomes guardian angel to another pilot (Van Johnson) courting his old girlfriend (Irene Dunne).
programme.title: A Guy Named Joe

programme.desc: Two Wild West con men (John Astin, Steve Carlson) ride into a Colorado town, unaware it sits on a fortune in ore.
programme.title: The Brothers O'Toole

programme.desc: A Harvard man (Robert Young) and his Colonial friend (Walter Brennan) join Rogers' (Spencer Tracy) Rangers in upstate New York.
programme.title: Northwest Passage

programme.desc: An Army deserter (Ray Milland) joins a gambler (Barbara Stanwyck) and a miner (Barry Fitzgerald) against a former slave-trader building a private empire.
programme.title: California

programme.desc: A reporter hears how an ex-convict (James Caan) and an Indian woman (Stefanie Powers) destroyed an Old West town overnight.
programme.title: Gone With the West

programme.desc: The writer's (Richard Chamberlain) romance with his wife, Zelda (Blythe Danner), inspires the story of a soldier and a flirt in 1919 Alabama.
programme.title: F. Scott Fitzgerald and the Last of the Belles

programme.desc: During the Civil War, reports of a sea monster attacking ships alarm the Navy. An investigation finds the monster is a submarine, the "Nautilus," commanded by vengeance-seeking Capt. Nemo (Allen Holubar), who was once an Indian prince.
programme.title: 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea

programme.desc: The story of the heroic English nurse who was executed by a German firing squad for aiding refugees during World War I.
programme.title: Nurse Edith Cavell

programme.desc: A U.S. senator's (Victor Moore) probe of corruption leads him to Mardi Gras, a fall guy (Bob Hope) and a filibuster.
programme.title: Louisiana Purchase

programme.desc: A Broadway producer (Zero Mostel) and his accountant (Gene Wilder) back a sure-fire flop: "Springtime for Hitler."
programme.title: The Producers

programme.desc: Two guys (Chuck Norris, Louis Gossett Jr.) and a girl (Melody Anderson) brave jungle hazards to find an Aztec temple filled with gold.
programme.title: Firewalker

programme.desc: A lusty Greek peasant (Anthony Quinn) shows a British writer (Alan Bates) how to live and run a lignite mine.
programme.title: Zorba the Greek

programme.desc: Three bumbling janitors encounter a slew of curious creatures after accidentally launching a rocket to Venus.
programme.title: Have Rocket, Will Travel

programme.desc: A Broadway star (Bette Davis) takes a young and seemingly naive aspiring actress (Anne Baxter) under her wing.
programme.title: All About Eve

programme.desc: Agents of heaven and hell fight for Little Joe's (Eddie "Rochester" Anderson) soul after his wife (Ethel Waters) gets him a pardon.
programme.title: Cabin in the Sky

programme.desc: Three retired Texas Rangers rescue a former colleague and eventually become the sheriff and deputies of Waco.
programme.title: The Over-the-Hill Gang Rides Again

programme.desc: Gen. Douglas MacArthur (Gregory Peck) returns to the Philippines, manages postwar Japan and defies President Truman (Ed Flanders).
programme.title: MacArthur

programme.desc: The mythical Greek hero (Reg Park) rescues a maiden left at the mercy of a beast and goes on to save his son from the sexist queen of lost Atlantis (Fay Spain).
programme.title: Hercules and the Captive Women

programme.desc: Supercomputer HAL 9000 guides astronauts (Keir Dullea, Gary Lockwood, William Sylvester) on a trip to find the origins of humans.
programme.title: 2001: A Space Odyssey

programme.desc: The sons (Humphrey Bogart, William Holden) of a Long Island tycoon become romantic rivals for the chauffeur's daughter (Audrey Hepburn).
programme.title: Sabrina

programme.desc: An agent (Tom Ewell) turns the girlfriend (Jayne Mansfield) of a former gangster (Edmond O'Brien) into a rock 'n' roll star.
programme.title: The Girl Can't Help It

programme.desc: Sudden money ruins a struggling writer (Van Johnson) and his wife (Elizabeth Taylor) in post-World War II Paris.
programme.title: The Last Time I Saw Paris

programme.desc: Clumsy Inspector Jacques Clouseau (Peter Sellers) chases a jewel thief (David Niven) and his nephew (Robert Wagner).
programme.title: The Pink Panther

programme.desc: In order to help bring Nazis to justice, a U.S. agent brings a traitor's daughter to Rio de Janeiro to seduce an exiled Nazi.
programme.title: Notorious

programme.desc: Batman (Adam West) and Robin (Burt Ward) save Gotham City from the Catwoman, Joker (Cesar Romero), Penguin and Riddler.
programme.title: Batman

programme.desc: A poor Chicago secretary (Diana Ross) takes the fast lane to fame as a model and a fashion designer.
programme.title: Mahogany

programme.desc: An electrician (Richard Hatch), a TV newswoman (Kay Lenz) and a mad scientist enter a dimension ruled by a warlord (John Saxon).
programme.title: Prisoners of the Lost Universe

programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes (Basil Rathbone) and Dr. Watson (Nigel Bruce) prowl foggy moors to save Sir Henry (Richard Greene) from a killer-dog family curse.
programme.title: The Hound of the Baskervilles

programme.desc: A paratrooper's (Dean Martin) goofy buddy (Jerry Lewis) joins him on base and makes a mockery of maneuvers.
programme.title: Jumping Jacks

programme.desc: Several would-be heirs are forced to spend a night in a haunted house in order to collect their inheritance.
programme.title: The Cat and the Canary

programme.desc: The miserly head (Brian Donlevy) of a Hollywood studio hires a goofy paperhanger (Jerry Lewis) to spy on his employees.
programme.title: The Errand Boy

programme.desc: Controversial British officer T.E. Lawrence (Peter O'Toole) learns the culture of Arabs (Alec Guinness, Anthony Quinn) and unites their tribes against the Turks.
programme.title: Lawrence of Arabia

programme.desc: A California girl called Gidget (Sandra Dee) spends summer with surf bums called Moondoggie (James Darren) and Kahuna (Cliff Robertson).
programme.title: Gidget

programme.desc: A detective (Peter O'Toole) helps a French art forger's daughter (Audrey Hepburn) steal a fake Venus.
programme.title: How to Steal a Million

programme.desc: Sailor Sinbad (John Phillip Law) fights a one-eyed centaur, a ship's figurehead and other creatures sent by an evil prince (Tom Baker).
programme.title: The Golden Voyage of Sinbad

programme.desc: A cartoonist (Dean Martin) turns his babbling roommate's (Jerry Lewis) bizarre dreams into a hit comic strip read by spies.
programme.title: Artists and Models

programme.desc: A corporate climber (Jack Lemmon), whose boss (Fred MacMurray) and others use his apartment for hanky-panky, aids a young woman (Shirley MacLaine).
programme.title: The Apartment

programme.desc: A heroin addict is caught between two Times Square narcotics agents and the pusher they are trying to bust.
programme.title: Born to Win

programme.desc: The queen of Egypt (Elizabeth Taylor) seduces Julius Caesar (Rex Harrison), but when he is killed, she uses Mark Antony (Richard Burton) as her new protector.
programme.title: Cleopatra
